steps.title: Identify the structures at the sentence boundary
steps.content: Left: independent clause ending with “study its ancient tombs.” Right: participial phrase “built into the rocky outcrops of a vast desert” followed by independent clause “these burial chambers seem to blend seamlessly with nature.”

steps.title: Apply the comma splice rule
steps.content: Two independent clauses cannot be joined by a comma alone—this violates the comma splice rule. Option A correctly separates them with a period, allowing the participial phrase to introduce the second sentence. Options B and D create comma splices by joining independent clauses with only a comma; option C produces an incoherent parallel structure. That matches choice A — A is correct.
